Document Title: Network Based Machine Learning Description of the Antidepressive ADR Landscape
Authors: A. K. M. Ushijima; G. de Anda–Jáuregui; E. Hernández–Lemus
Author Affiliations: Computational Genomics Division, National Institute of Genomic Medicine, Mexico City, Mexico; National Institute of Genomic Medicine, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México, Consejo Nacional de Humanidades, Ciencias y Tecnologías, Mexico City, Mexico; National Institute of Genomic Medicine, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de, México, Mexico City, Mexico
Publication Title: 2024 13th International Conference On Software Process Improvement (CIMPS)
Abstract: Depression is a common mental disorder that affects the lives of millions of people around the world. Antidepressants are used to treat this disorder, whoever they lead to adverse drug reactions (ADRs). Adverse drug reactions (ADRs) are harmful or unpleasant reactions produced by an intervention when using pharmacological substances. Studies have correlated the nonadherence of antidepressant treatments with ADRs. It is important to predict potential ADRs and create therapies that reduce ADRs to improve adherence from the patient. Here, we applied methods -using knowledge discovery in the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) Adverse Event Reporting System (FAERS) database, graph embeddings of the chemical structure of the drugs, and machine learning methods (random forest and XGBoost) - to predict potential adverse drug reactions. We analyzed a set of 28 antidepressant drugs and identified two main features that were significant in predicting ADRs when comparing with other antidepressants.
Author Keywords: adverse drug reactions;graph embedding;graph2vec;antidepressants;random forest regression;XGBoost regression

Document Title: A Reasoning System for Graded Modal Logic
Author Affiliations: IIMAS: Posgrado en Ciencia e lngeniería de la Computación, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México, Mexico City, Mexico; Facultad de Ingenería: Dpto. de Computación, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México, Mexico City, Mexico
Publication Title: 2024 12th International Conference in Software Engineering Research and Innovation (CONISOFT)
Abstract: In this paper, we propose a deduction system for the modal logic $\mathbf{K}$ with graded modalities, which is obtained by adding numerical constraints to the modal operators. Graded modalities can be seen as a generalization of traditional modal operators since they include numerical bounds in the modalities, thus enabling the formal treatment of more detailed expressions, such as “there are at least $n+1$ individuals satisfying a certain property”, where $n$ is a natural number. This kind of properties commonly arise in contexts such as concurrent systems: “if at least 3 processors are free, then proceed to compute the next task”, or epistemic logic:”if more than 2 people know $P$, then $P$ is known”. The system is based on the notion of tree-hypersequents. We show the system is correct, i.e. sound and complete.
IEEE Terms: Technological innovation;Interpolation;Program processors;Computational modeling;Syntactics;Cognition;Numerical models;Logic;Iterative methods;Formal verification

Document Title: Democratizing Air Quality Monitoring: The Role of Low-Cost PM2.5 Sensors in Community Decision- Making
Authors: A. Castro-Chacon; A. Castro-Carranza; M. Grutter; R. Torres-Jardón
Author Affiliations: Postgraduate Progam in Earth Sciences, ENES Morelia, National Autonomous University of Mexico, Morelia, Mexico; International Laboratory of Environmental Electron Devices (LAIDEA) ENES Morelia, National Autonomous University of Mexico, Morelia, Mexico; Institute of Atmospheric Sciences and Climate Change (ICAyCC), National Autonomous University of Mexico, Mexico City; Institute of Atmospheric Sciences and Climate Change (ICAyCC), National Autonomous University of Mexico, Mexico City
Publication Title: 2025 IEEE Latin American Electron Devices Conference (LAEDC)
Abstract: Low-cost PM2.5 sensors have appeared as valuable tools for expanding access to air quality information in underserved regions. This paper explores the operational principles of sensors like the Plantower PMS5003, focusing on data acquisition, processing, and communication strategies. Beyond technical aspects, we discuss their integration into community-based monitoring and how these microelectronic systems support environmental governance and strengthen local responses to air pollution. Finally, this work highlights low-cost air quality sensors as humanitarian technologies that can help connect scientific knowledge, societal empowerment, and policy development, particularly in the Latin American context.

Document Title: Transforming Software Development: A Study on the Integration of Multi-Agent Systems and Large Language Models for Automatic Code Generation
Authors: R. Ramírez-Rueda; E. Benítez-Guerrero; C. Mezura-Godoy; E. Bárcenas
Author Affiliations: Facultad de Estadística e Informática, Universidad veracruzana, Xalapa, Mexico; Facultad de Estadística e Informática, Universidad veracruzana, Xalapa, Mexico; Facultad de Estadística e Informática, Universidad veracruzana, Xalapa, Mexico; Facultad de Ingeniería, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México, Ciudad de México, México
Publication Title: 2024 12th International Conference in Software Engineering Research and Innovation (CONISOFT)
Abstract: This paper explores the integration of Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) and Large Language Models (LLMs) for auto-matic code generation, addressing the limitations of traditional manual coding. By conducting a comprehensive review of existing literature and analyzing a practical case study, we demonstrate how MAS and LLMs can collaboratively enhance software development processes. The research focuses on the technical and theoretical challenges of this integration, highlighting the potential for improved productivity, adaptability, and quality in code generation. The findings contribute to AI-based software engineering by revealing new research directions in collective intelligence and automated programming.
IEEE Terms: Productivity;Technological innovation;Codes;Large language models;Collective intelligence;Software;Standards;Software development management;Software engineering;Multi-agent systems

Document Title: Using the Git Version Control System to Replace a Learning Management System
Author Affiliations: Facultad de Ingeniería (FI) and Instituto de Investigaciones Económicas (IIEc), Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México (UNAM), Coyoacán, Ciudad de México, Mexico
Publication Title: IEEE Revista Iberoamericana de Tecnologias del Aprendizaje
Abstract: For several years now, but with special force since the beginning of the COVID contingency, higher education professors have turned to telematic tools that help teachers manage the work with which their students practice and demonstrate their progress in class– Tasks, practices, projects, exhibitions and more. The systems that, over the last two decades, have been most used for this purpose are called Learning Management Systems (LMS); The best known is undoubtedly Moodle, but there are a large number of options for this. Based on the empirical observation of a rejection by students of the use of these systems, the authors made the decision to completely replace their use with that of a version control system widely used for software development, Git, specifically for teaching students of the Computer Engineering career, for the Operating Systems course. This article presents the justification for choosing this platform, and the evaluation of the experience after eight semesters using it.
Author Keywords: Computer applications;learning technologies;version control;learning management systems
IEEE Terms: Software development management;Education;Surveys;Source coding;Operating systems;Computer applications;Learning systems;Learning management systems

Document Title: XR for educational anatomy: an Immersive human atlas with chatbot
Authors: J. -F. Uhl; P. Reyes; C. Quihuis; P. Belchior; J. Jorge
Author Affiliations: Director of the UNESCO Chair of Digital Anatomy, Paris Cité University, Paris, France; Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México, México City, México; Instituto Tecnológico y de Estudios Superiores de Monterrey, Mexico City, México; INESC-ID, Tagus Park, Porto Salvo, Portugal; UNESCO Chair on AI & VR, Instituto Superior Técnico, Av Rovisco Pais, Lisboa, Portugal
Publication Title: 2025 IEEE Conference on Virtual Reality and 3D User Interfaces Abstracts and Workshops (VRW)
Abstract: Immersive reality has emerged as a groundbreaking and promising approach for teaching anatomy, providing unique learning experiences while complementing traditional educational methods. Existing immersive software, such as Organon® and HoloLens®, offer valuable tools but remain limited in anatomical detail and interactivity. We present a novel educational approach: an immersive human atlas that integrates anatomical slices from the Visible Korean Human dataset (male and female) into a 3D environment. This platform enhances anatomical visualization and incorporates a Large Language Model (LLM) to support user interaction and understanding. By combining precise anatomical slices within the 3D model, the atlas provides an enriched and comprehensive learning experience for medical education.
Funding Information: Fundação para a Ciência e a Tecnologia;
Author Keywords: Extended Reality;Immersive Environments;Anatomy Education;Large Language Models;Cross Computing
IEEE Terms: Visualization;Solid modeling;Three-dimensional displays;Extended reality;Large language models;Conferences;Education;User interfaces;Software;Anatomy
